name: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI)
description: Acute kidney injury is sudden onset loss of renal function due to toxins (grapes, raisins, NSAIDs, lily ingestion, ethylene glycol), infections (leptospirosis), or ischaemia. Unlike CKD, recovery is possible if treated rapidly.
treatment_overview: Emergency IV fluid diuresis, correction of electrolyte abnormalities, anti-nausea drugs, and renal diet. Dialysis may be needed in refractory cases. Identify and eliminate the underlying cause.
prevention_tips: Keep grapes, raisins, and toxic plants away from dogs
Dose NSAIDs carefully and avoid in dehydrated dogs
